serving.natural_language_portion: A typical serving of Jumbo Clam Gourmet Porridge is one bowl, weighing approximately 500 grams.

nutrient_source.title: Health Promotion Board Singapore — Nutrient Composition of Food
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values are adapted from HPB Singapore for 'Porridge with Seafood' and 'Clams', adjusted for the specific dish description.

gi_source.title: International Tables of Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load Values: 2021
gi_source.source_match_notes: GI for white rice porridge (congee) is estimated at 80. This dish is primarily white rice porridge with clams, which do not significantly alter the GI of the carbohydrate base.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content from broth and fish sauce.
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should consume this dish sparingly due to its high sodium content (240mg/100g); request reduced salt and fish sauce, and consider pairing with low-sodium foods.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High glycemic index of rice porridge and low fiber content.
type2_diabetes.guidance: Due to the high glycemic index of rice porridge, individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should consume a very small portion, pair it with additional protein and non-starchy vegetables, and monitor blood glucose levels closely.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Borderline sodium content and presence of cholesterol.
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: While low in saturated and trans fats, the borderline sodium content (240mg/100g) and cholesterol (14mg/100g) warrant moderate consumption for individuals with cardiovascular disease; opt for reduced sodium preparation.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Anti-inflammatory properties of ginger and beneficial minerals from clams, but high GI rice base.
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: The ginger and minerals from clams may offer some anti-inflammatory benefits, but portion control is advised due to the high GI rice base which could potentially contribute to inflammation; request reduced fish sauce to manage sodium intake.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Jumbo Clam Gourmet Porridge is a comforting Chinese-style dish, popular in Singapore, featuring tender jumbo clams simmered to perfection in a savory, often ginger-infused, rice broth. The porridge has a smooth, creamy texture from the long-cooked rice, contrasting with the delicate chewiness of the clams. It is typically seasoned with soy sauce, fish sauce, and white pepper, offering a rich umami flavor. Nutritionally, it provides protein from the clams but is high in carbohydrates due to the rice base, leading to a high glycemic index. It can also be high in sodium, which is a concern for individuals with hypertension. The dish is often consumed for breakfast or as a light, easily digestible meal, especially when feeling unwell.

confidence_reason: Nutrient data is adapted from HPB Singapore for similar dishes (seafood porridge, clams) and adjusted based on the specific dish description. GI is an analogous estimate for white rice porridge. Multilingual names are generated based on common translations for Singaporean cuisine.

Trans_fat_g is confirmed zero as it is not deep-fried. Vitamin_a_mcg_rae and vitamin_d_mcg are naturally zero for this dish.
